title: methods.rs: SetResponse<T> generic + filter_kind: Option<ChatKind>
description:
What: Two type improvements to methods.rs: (1) make SetResponse generic over the created/updated object type; (2) change chat_query's filter_kind from Option<&str> to Option<ChatKind>.

Algorithm:

PART 1 — SetResponse<T>:
1. Change the SetResponse struct in methods.rs:
   - Add type parameter T to the struct
   - Add #[serde(bound(deserialize = "T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned"))] before the struct
   - Change fields: created: Option<HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>> → created: Option<HashMap<String, T>>
   - Change fields: updated: Option<HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>> → updated: Option<HashMap<String, T>>
   - Keep other fields (account_id, old_state, new_state, destroyed, not_created, not_updated, not_destroyed) unchanged.
2. Update method return types in methods.rs:
   - message_create returns Result<SetResponse<serde_json::Value>, ClientError> (serde_json::Value is safe fallback for now)
   - read_position_set returns Result<SetResponse<serde_json::Value>, ClientError>
3. In methods_test.rs, add type annotations where the type inference needs help:
   - Any let result = client.message_create(...).await? needs to become let result: SetResponse<serde_json::Value> = ... or have the type inferred from usage.
   - The test at line ~95 accesses result.created[key]["id"] — Value indexing still works with T=Value.

PART 2 — filter_kind: Option<ChatKind>:
1. Change chat_query signature: filter_kind: Option<&str> → filter_kind: Option<ChatKind>
2. In the method body, replace:
   if let Some(k) = filter_kind { filter.insert("kind".into(), k.into()); }
   with:
   if let Some(k) = filter_kind {
       let kind_str = serde_json::to_value(&k).map_err(|e| ClientError::Serialize)?;
       filter.insert("kind".into(), kind_str);
   }
   Note: serde_json::to_value(&ChatKind::Direct) produces Value::String("direct") due to #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")].
3. Remove the #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] from chat_query if it is present (check first).
4. Call site in client_task.rs: passes None → no change needed (Option<ChatKind> accepts None).
5. Run: cargo test -p jmap-chat

Defensive rules:
- Do NOT change any other method signatures.
- Do NOT change the Serialize bound — only Deserialize needs the bound override.
- serde_json::to_value for ChatKind must not panic; if it errors (it won't), propagate as ClientError::Serialize.

Tests:
- SetResponse<serde_json::Value> deserializes existing message_create fixture correctly.
- chat_query with None filter_kind still works.
- chat_query with Some(ChatKind::Direct) serializes kind:"direct" in the filter object.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p jmap-chat passes. cargo clippy -p jmap-chat -- -D warnings passes. SetResponse<T> compiles. filter_kind accepts Option<ChatKind>.
